tool_args.label: NYCT Cash Deficit vs Final Estimate Timing Items
tool_args.raw_text: NYCT's cash deficit of $5,132.0 million was $20.0 million unfavorable compared with the Final Estimate...

payload.claim_text: NYCT's cash deficit of $5,132.0 million was $20.0 million unfavorable compared with the Final Estimate, primarily due to shortfalls in farebox revenue of $6.5 million and other operating revenues of $27.5 million, capital and other reimbursable of $250.9 million mostly offset by lower operating expenses of $264.9 million. These variances include significant timing-related items, including $107.8 million of underruns in Subway Action Plan (SAP) expenses and the entire shortfall in capital reimbursements. Excluding these timing-related items, the NYCT cash position was $58.4 million favorable compared with the Final Estimate.
payload.subject: NYCT cash deficit

tool_args.label: 2022 Ridership Recovery Context
tool_args.raw_text: Overall, 2022 combined subway and bus ridership was 27% higher than 2021 but just 59% of the 2019 pre-COVID-19 level 7 .

payload.claim_text: Overall, 2022 combined subway and bus ridership was 27% higher than 2021 but just 59% of the 2019 pre-COVID-19 level.
payload.subject: combined subway and bus ridership

tool_args.label: 2022 Non-Reimbursable Overtime Variance Breakdown
tool_args.raw_text: Overall increase of $101.5M or 16.2 percent compared to YTD Dec 2021...

payload.claim_text: Non-reimbursable overtime overall increase of $101.5M or 16.2 percent compared to YTD Dec 2021. Subways $12.4M overrun: $11.9M for severe weather response; $3.0M for additional maintenance requirements; $0.6M due to vacancy coverage requirements; -$3.1M COVID-19 response underrun. Buses -$15.3M underrun: -$12.4M COVID-19 response; -$7.1M for vacancy, availability and maintenance coverage offset by overrun of $2.8M for severe weather response; $1.4M for service operations requirement.
payload.subject: non-reimbursable overtime

tool_args.label: Bus Network Redesign Timing Variance
tool_args.raw_text: Professional Service Contracts of $148.2 million was $26.9 million or 15.3% favorable to the Final Estimate...

payload.claim_text: Professional Service Contracts of $148.2 million was $26.9 million or 15.3% favorable to the Final Estimate, reflecting a favorable timing variance of $16.5 million consisting of the timing of legal billings of $8.0 million, the Bus Network Redesign project of $3.0 million, other projects of $4.0 million and a real underrun of $10.4 million in professional service payments.
payload.subject: Professional Service Contracts - Bus Network Redesign

tool_args.label: Subway Action Plan (SAP) Timing Underruns

payload.subject: Subway Action Plan (SAP) expenses

tool_args.label: Federal American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA) Accrual
tool_args.raw_text: Other operating revenue of $5,367 million was $4,849 million higher than the Adopted Budget, primarily reflecting the accrued recognition of the $4,850 million from the Federal American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA) provided by the Federal Government.

payload.claim_text: Other operating revenue of $5,367 million was $4,849 million higher than the Adopted Budget, primarily reflecting the accrued recognition of the $4,850 million from the Federal American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA) provided by the Federal Government.
payload.subject: ARPA federal COVID aid
